subject: Human Resources
title: Turning Data into Action
description: A quick, practical guide to Turning Data into Action.

hook.text: A company surveys 10,000 employees. Beautiful dashboards are made. Nothing changes. The data dies in a slide deck.

buildup.text: Engagement data is useless without action plans—specific changes tied to specific findings on a specific timeline.

discovery.text: The companies that improve engagement share results transparently and commit to 2-3 focused actions—not 20.

twist.text: Taking no action after a survey is worse than not surveying—it tells employees their voice doesn't matter.

climax.text: Small, visible changes build trust. Employees don't need perfection—they need proof that feedback leads somewhere.

punchline.text: Surveys without action are just expensive silence.

quiz.question: Why is inaction after an engagement survey harmful?
options: It signals to employees that their feedback doesn't matter
It gives HR more time to analyze
